nodes.label: Attribution-Based Hierarchical Diffusion
nodes.rationale: draft5's central learning mechanism: every hierarchy level (Scap to Limbic Loop) stores distribution memory of how much each child contributed on the forward pass; loss diffuses top-down in proportion to stored contribution, measured for free as the analog forward current |a·W|. Positioned explicitly against gradient descent - it routes credit by measured contribution, not by chain-rule sensitivity, so it never computes or transports a true gradient.

nodes.label: Scap (Synapse Capacitor)
nodes.rationale: The leaf-level analog weight cell: a capacitor holding magnitude, an SRAM sign bit, plus Forward_Sign and Momentum SRAM. A Scap is explicitly a wire, not a neuron - its forward current already encodes pre x post. The only structural element that survives unchanged from draft 1 into draft 6.0.

nodes.label: Ganglion (2-3-3-2 topology, locked)
nodes.rationale: The atomic composition unit: 29 Scaps wired as a hardwired 2-3-3-2 op-amp network (2 in, 3 hidden L2, 3 hidden L3, 2 out). Locked per §22 #1. Ganglions chain into Columns, branch into Lobes, top out at a Brainstem.

nodes.label: Current Mirror (analog share divider)
nodes.rationale: The recommended analog op-amp topology for computing child_contribution/total_contribution in hardware: a parent's total loss current is forced into a central node and branched out to children in proportion to measured |a·W|, so the division is ratio-preserving even under thermal/supply drift. This is the circuit that performs hierarchical diffusion's normalization step.

nodes.label: Forward_Sign_SRAM
nodes.rationale: A per-Scap latch of sign(input_a) XOR Sign_SRAM, i.e. the sign of that Scap's own a·W product on the last forward pass - distinct from Sign_SRAM (the sign of the weight itself). Only carries new information for signed activations (9 of 29 Scaps per Ganglion under ReLU); exists specifically to resolve multi-parent direction conflict in §9.4.

nodes.label: Dead-Weight Collapse
nodes.rationale: Known failure mode #1 of attribution-based learning: because ΔW ∝ |a·W|, a weight that reaches zero magnitude receives zero update and stays dead forever. Mitigated by the floor-at-1 momentum rule and residual bypass connections.

nodes.label: Forward_Sign XOR Feedback Update Rule
nodes.rationale: The per-Scap backward update: direction = Forward_Sign_SRAM XOR feedback; weight_cap -= pulse_width x momentum x direction. Worked example in §3.7 computes '+ XOR + = -' for one concrete case. Root CLAUDE.md later names this exact section pair (§3.3/§3.7) as the site of a sign bug that 'lived four drafts because nobody computed + XOR +' - the mechanical seed of the broader missing-sign collapse.

nodes.label: Activity vs Relevance Gap
nodes.rationale: Known failure mode #6: high Scap activity (|a·W|) does not necessarily mean high relevance to the actual loss - a Scap can be very active but cancelled out downstream, yet still claim a large loss share. Long-tail low-activity/high-relevance features get under-updated; Physical Saturation is the primary defense.
